title: Listening - Part 1
description: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best.
instructions: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear. There are two questions for each extract.

extracts.context: You hear two friends discussing a new historical podcast they’ve been listening to.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Have you heard the latest episode of 'Past-Perfect'? The one about the Great Fire of London was gripping. || Man: [1]I found the host's habit of using modern slang a bit jarring[/1], to be honest. It didn't really fit the historical theme. || Woman: I see what you mean. It was a bit too 'informal' at times. || Man: [2]What I really liked, though, was the use of immersive sound effects[/2]. You could almost feel the heat of the flames. || Woman: I agree. The production value is top-notch. || Man: Exactly. It’s a great way to learn about history, as long as you can ignore the constant 'Gen-Z' references.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Have you heard the latest episode of 'Past-Perfect'? The one about the Great Fire of London was gripping.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I found the host's habit of using modern slang a bit jarring, to be honest. It didn't really fit the historical theme.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I see what you mean. It was a bit too 'informal' at times.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: What I really liked, though, was the use of immersive sound effects. You could almost feel the heat of the flames.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I agree. The production value is top-notch.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Exactly. It’s a great way to learn about history, as long as you can ignore the constant 'Gen-Z' references.

questions.question: What is the man's main criticism of the podcast?

options.A: the inaccuracy of the facts presented
options.B: the linguistic style of the presenter
options.C: the poor audio quality of the recording

questions.explanation: He finds the host's 'modern slang' and 'Gen-Z references' to be jarring [1].

questions.question: Both speakers admired the podcast's
questions.explanation: They both praise the 'immersive sound effects' and 'production value' [2].

extracts.context: You hear two colleagues talking about a new city-centre parking scheme.
extracts.audio_script: Man: So, the new 'Green-Zone' parking is active. Have you managed to find a spot yet? || Woman: [3]I suspect the city council is just using 'environmentalism' as a cover to increase the price of parking tickets[/3]. || Man: It is remarkably expensive now. I’ve started taking the bus. || Woman: [4]I’ve noticed that the new charging stations for electric cars are often blocked by regular vehicles[/4], which seems a bit poorly managed. || Man: I agree. If they don't enforce the rules, the whole system falls apart. || Woman: Exactly. It’s a nice idea in theory, but it’s becoming more of a headache for commuters than a benefit for the planet.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: So, the new 'Green-Zone' parking is active. Have you managed to find a spot yet?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I suspect the city council is just using 'environmentalism' as a cover to increase the price of parking tickets.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: It is remarkably expensive now. I’ve started taking the bus.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I’ve noticed that the new charging stations for electric cars are often blocked by regular vehicles, which seems a bit poorly managed.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. If they don't enforce the rules, the whole system falls apart.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. It’s a nice idea in theory, but it’s becoming more of a headache for commuters than a benefit for the planet.

questions.question: The woman believes the parking scheme was motivated by

options.A: a genuine desire to reduce pollution.
options.B: a need to generate more revenue.

questions.explanation: She thinks 'environmentalism' is a cover to 'increase the price of parking tickets' [3].

questions.question: What practical issue do both speakers identify with the scheme?

options.A: the lack of available parking spaces
options.B: the poor maintenance of the machines
options.C: the ineffective regulation of the area

questions.explanation: They both note that charging stations are blocked and rules aren't enforced [4].

extracts.context: You hear two people discussing a recently released documentary about artificial intelligence.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I watched 'The Silicone Mind' last night. It was quite a terrifying look at where we’re heading. || Woman: [5]I found the constant fast-paced editing and dramatic music a bit exhausting[/5], to be honest. It felt like it was trying to scare me rather than inform me. || Man: I see your point. It was a bit sensationalist. || Woman: [6]I did appreciate the interviews with the actual researchers, though[/6]. They gave a much more balanced view than the narrator did. || Man: I agree. Their perspective was much more grounded in reality. || Woman: Definitely. It’s worth watching for their insights, even if the rest of the film is a bit 'Hollywood'.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I watched 'The Silicone Mind' last night. It was quite a terrifying look at where we’re heading.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I found the constant fast-paced editing and dramatic music a bit exhausting, to be honest. It felt like it was trying to scare me rather than inform me.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I see your point. It was a bit sensationalist.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I did appreciate the interviews with the actual researchers, though. They gave a much more balanced view than the narrator did.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. Their perspective was much more grounded in reality.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Definitely. It’s worth watching for their insights, even if the rest of the film is a bit 'Hollywood'.

questions.question: What was the woman's main issue with the documentary?

options.B: the manipulative nature of the style
options.C: the poor quality of the animation

questions.explanation: She finds the editing and music 'exhausting' and feels it's 'trying to scare me' rather than inform [5].

questions.question: Both speakers were impressed by the film's
questions.explanation: They both highlight the value and balance of the 'interviews with the actual researchers' [6].
